title: Cadillac outqualifies Aston Martin for the first time at F1 Japanese GP
summary:
Cadillac beat Aston Martin to 19th and 20th on the grid of Formula 1’s Japanese Grand Prix, on a tricky weekend for Sergio Perez.
Perez was the faster Cadillac driver on Saturday, with a 1m32.206s putting him 0.440s ahead of Aston Martin’s Fernando Alonso, albeit 1.1s away from 18th-placed Oliver Bearman and 1.3s down on the Q2 cut-off time.

title: Furious Charles Leclerc blasts F1 as Max Verstappen hits new low
summary:
Kimi Antonelli made it back-to-back pole positions after cruising to P1 in qualifying for the Japanese Grand Prix, highlighting his newfound confidence after winning in China.

Antonelli found himself on top of the world, while Charles Leclerc hit out at F1's new regulations and how bad it has made hot laps, branding it a "f****** joke". Max Verstappen's struggles also continued, as the Dutchman was eliminated in Q2 and starts 11th at Suzuka.

summary:
The new-generation of Formula 1 cars have changed how Suzuka’s first sector is tackled: beyond the reduced downforce, which lowers cornering speeds, drivers now barely touch the brake pedal, because the hybrid system decelerates the car through transitions to maximise energy recovery in a key section.
